LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS VWR INTERNATIONAL, INC., UNIVAR USA, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS, OR ALTERNATIVELY, STAY THE ACTION ON GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT OREGON IS A SUITABLE FORUM GIVEN THAT THE CALIFORNIA DEFENDANTS NOT SUBJECT TO OREGON JURISDICTION ARE A SIGNIFICANT NUMBER OF THE DEFENDANT IN THIS CASE. IN ANY EVENT, THE COURT WOULD DENY THE MOTION BECAUSE BALANCING ALL OF THE RELEVANT FACTORS, CALIFORNIA IS NOT AN INAPPROPRIATE FORUM ON THE FACTS OF THE CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON JUL-24-2007 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENTIAL SETTING IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 9/24/07 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR 9/17/07 @ 10:00AM IN DEPT. 220. FURTHER SETTLEMENT CONFERENCES SET FOR 9/19/07, 9/20/07 AND 9/21/07. PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND DUE BY 9/10/07; DEFENDANTS' COUNTER DEMAND DUE BY 9/13/07. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. ANSWER TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES WITHIN 20 DAYS OF SERVICE. DEFENDANT MAY REVIEW PRIOR DEPOSITION TRANSCRIPTS AND DISCOVERY RESPONSES AT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S OFFICE ON 7/27/07. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 8/24/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 9/6/07. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: DLOK; REPORTER: RITA ARLENE

Off Calendar Sep-14-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: ATTY. STEVEN J. PATTI (CLAPPER, PATTI, SCHWEIZER & MASON, 415.332.4262) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFFS.
ATTY. THEODORE E. BACON (ADORNO YOSS ALVARADO & SMITH, 714.852.6800) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT LAGUNA CLAY CO.'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS AT LEAST A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT ASSUMED LIABILITY PER INJURIES INCLUDING PLAINTIFF'S OCCURRING AFTER ITS PURCHASE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 437C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 (301/PJB/JF)

MOTION To Dismiss, Or Alternatively, Stay The Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Jul-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. STEVEN J. PATTI OF CLAPPER, PATTI, SCHWEIZER & MASON, P.C., TEL. NO. 332-4262 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NEVIN BROWNFIELD OF THELEN, REID, BROWN, RAYSMAN & STEINER, TEL. NO. 369-7784 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT VWR INTERNATIONAL INC, UNIVAR USA INC;
ATTY. KELLY L. COWAN OF ADAMS, NYE, SINUNU & BECHT, TEL. NO. 982-8955 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT WWC CORPORATION;
ATTY. DAN SULLIVAN OF GLASY AND GLASY, TEL. NO. (925)947-1300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT LONESTAR INDUSTRIES INC.
ATTY. STEVEN ALAN BEILOCK OF ADAMS, NYE, SINUNU, TEL. NO. 982-8955 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CHEMICAL DISTRIBUTORS INC.

RULING - A R G U E D ; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT V W R INTERNATIONAL, INC., UNIVAR USA INC. MOTION To Dismiss, Or Alternatively, Stay The Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens is DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT CARRIED IT'S BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT OREGON IS A SUITABLE FORUM GIVEN THAT THE CALIFORNIA DEFENDANTS NOT SUBJECT TO OREGON JURISDICTION ARE A SIGNIFICANT NUMBER OF THE DEFENDANT IN THIS CASE. IN ANY EVENT, THE COURT WOULD DENY THE MOTION BECAUSE BALANCING ALL OF THE RELEVANT FACTORS, CALIFORNIA IS NOT AN INAPPROPRIATE FORUM ON THE FACTS OF THE CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189 =301/PJB/JF
